label: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 2

extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam won from a perfect VAN POPPEL Danny leadout, benefiting from PEDERSEN rubbing with McLAY and losing rhythm in the sprint.
tactical_patterns: Alpecin-Fenix chased repeatedly for Wellens then failed to position him, wasting significant team energy before sprint finale.
Break caught 60km out signals teams no longer willing to gamble on intermediate distance escapes in flat Vuelta stages.
Time gaps declared in sprint finish awarded Teunissen red jersey, revealing positioning in final roundabouts created GC-relevant splits.

rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: Perfect leadout execution and timing suggests Bora-Hansgrohe sprint train superior to Alpecin despite Wellens favoritism
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: negative: Physical contact with McLay disrupted rhythm in finale, suggesting positioning vulnerability when shouldering occurs in tight sprints
rider_signals.WELLENS Tim: negative: Alpecin chased all day for him yet failed to deliver positioning, questioning team's sprint train coordination ability
rider_signals.VAN POPPEL Danny: positive: Delivered textbook leadout for Bennett, confirming role as elite sprint pilot despite lower individual results profile
